english: one that lives by carrying loads Tar. — 2. rarely porter, carrier of a load; k'úr-po (khur po) load, burden; k'úr-bu (khur bu), col. *k'úr-ru* (*khur ru*) prop. a small load; a load in general; k'úr-pá (khur pa) carrier, cooly; k'ur-rtsá (khur rtsa), k'ur-lám (khur lam) cooly-station, a day's journey, gen. 10 to 12 English miles; k'ur-rtsá-pa (khur rtsa pa) a station-cooly.

Cs.: bread, food, Sch. also forage, fodder. It is, however, not the common word for bread, but only for certain sorts, such as bra-k'úr (bra khur), bread of buckwheat, rtsabs-k'úr (rtsabs khur) q v., and more particularly it is applied to cakes and pastry-work baked in fat or oil. — 2. vb. v. _ok'úr-ba ('khur ba).
dandelion C., used as a pot-herb and medicinal plant; as the former it is also called k'ur-tsód (khur tshod).
C. and B. cheek, the ruddy part of the face below the eyes (cf. grám-pa (gram pa)); *k'ur-tsóg* (*khur tshog*) W.

1. Sch.: "the soft down of furs", abbreviation of k'ú-lu (khu lu); k'ul-mal (khul mal) small basket for wool Ts. — 2. ravine Kun. — 3. district, province, domain; lhá-sa k'ul (lha sa khul) all that belongs to Lhasa Georgi Alph., *dei k'ul-la dug* (*dei khul la dug*) is subject to him C.
the bottom, or the side of a thing Cs.

(Sch.: k'e-ma (khe ma)) 1. profit, gain; k'e-spógs (khe spogs) B. and C., *k'e-béd* (*khe bed*) W. id.; k'e-tsón byéd-pa (khe tshon byed pa) to trade, to traffic Pth.; śés-kyi k'e (shes kyi khe) gain, advantage obtained by knowledge and attainments; k'e-pa (khe pa) tradesman, dealer; tsóṅ-dus k'e-pa (tshong dus khe pa) trader in a market Mil.; k'e-nyén (khe nyen) Sch.; profit and loss, risk; k'e-sgrúb-pa (khe sgrub pa) Cs., *k'ye-béd tób-će* (*khye bed thob ce*) W., to make profit, to gain, k'e brgyáb-pa (khe brgyab pa), to make a good bargain Sch.; *k'e-ru do-wa* (*khe ru do wa*) C. to abate, to go down in price; *k'é-ćaṇ* (*khe can*), *k'e-mé* (*khe me*), profitable, unprofitable; *k'yé-mo* (*khye mo*) W. cheap. — 2. tetter, herpes, ringworm (eruption of the skin) Sch.

1. partic. of k'éṅs-pa (khengs pa), filled, replete with. — 2. adj. puffed up, proud, haughty, arrogant; sbst. pride etc.; k'eṅs-séms (khengs sems), k'eṅs-drégs (khengs dregs) pride. k'eṅs-po (khengs po) Med. with reference to food: producing flatulence.
1. Schr. worm-wood, prob. erron. for k'ám-pa (kham pa). — 2. Sch.: to lean, to repose on, erron. for bkán-pa (bkan pa).

col., W.: *k'yebs* (*khyebs*), Cs.: k'ébs-ma (khebs ma) covering, coverlet: *k'eb sáṅ-pa* (*kheb sang pa*); to take the covering off C.; čár-k'ebs (char khebs) a covering against rain, rain-cloak; stéṅ-k'ebs (steng khebs), lčóg-k'ebs (lcog khebs), table-cloth Cs.; tód-k'ebs (thod khebs) Lt. cap, hood; gdúṅ-k'ebs (gdung khebs), a certain beam or board above the capital of a pillar; gdóṅ-k'ebs (gdong khebs), veil, cloth to cover the head; *dúm-k'yebs* (*dum khyebs*) W. apron; *paṅ-k'éb* (*pang kheb*) C. napkin, apron.
prob. for k'él-ba (khel ba), to load upon; blo k'él-ba (blo khel ba) is said to be used in C. for blo skyél-ba (blo skyel ba) W., v. skyél-ba (skyel ba) no. 6.
C. to hit, _otsáms-la ('tshams la) (or ntsáms-la? (mtshams la?)) to hit the right thing, the exact point or line; gṇád-la (gnad la) to strike the vital parts, to hit mortally, fatally.
1. numeral, 122. — 2. Bal. (*χ'o* (*kho*)) for k'a-ba (kha ba), bitter.

pers. pron. of the third person, he, she, it, but almost exclusively in col. language. In ancient writings it occurs but rarely, being either omitted or supplied by de (de), but in later works that come nearer to the present language, it is to be found the more frequently. k'oi (khoi) his, her; *k'ó-pa* (*kho pa*), *k'ó-wa* (*kho wa*) plur. they, W. and C., v. Georgi Alph., in an edict; *k'ó-ćag* (*kho cag*), *k'ó-tso* (*kho tsho*) id. C.; *k'ó-wa nyi* (*kho wa nyi*) W., both of them: k'o-ráṅ (kho rang) 1. he himself. 2. he, = *k'o* (*kho*) col.; with partic.: k'o dá-ći sṅon la soṅ-ba de (kho da ci sngon la song ba de), Mil., he that just went on in advance, proceded in front. Note. The word prob. has been originally a sbst., denoting essence, substance
